Which of the following SAFe Lean-Agile principles involves delivering a continuous flow of value to customers in the shortest sustainable lead time?
Decentralized decision-making
Apply systems thinking
Take an economic view
Make value flow without interruptions

The SAFe Lean-Agile principle that involves delivering a continuous flow of value to customers in the shortest sustainable lead time is "Make value flow without interruptions". This principle focuses on optimizing the flow of value through the entire system and aims to reduce the time it takes from the initial concept to delivering value to the customer, minimizing delays, and eliminating bottlenecks.

Which of the following SAFe Core Competencies of Business Agility includes the Customer Centricity and Design Thinking dimension?
Lean Portfolio Management
Lean-Agile Leadership
Continuous Learning Culture
Agile Product Delivery

The SAFe Core Competency that includes the Customer Centricity and Design Thinking dimension is "Agile Product Delivery." This competency emphasizes understanding customer needs and solving customer problems through a continuous delivery pipeline that allows businesses to deliver value directly to customers. It underscores the importance of building a customer-centric culture where design thinking is utilized to create innovative solutions and ensure that the product or service meets the customer's requirements and expectations.

Transparency
〇〇〇
Respect for People
Built-In Quality
Alignment
Which of the following SAFe Core Values involves coaching aspiring developers to grow their skillsets and fill new roles throughout the

The SAFe Core Value that involves coaching aspiring developers to grow their skillsets and fill new roles throughout the organization is "Respect for People." This value emphasizes the importance of human capital in the workflow. It promotes personal respect, growth, and continuous development, recognizing that the encouragement and professional development of individuals is key to the success of the organization.

Which of the following methods for gathering customer feedback relies on building analytic systems to deliver information about how customers are using the Solution?
Refactoring
Telemetry
〇〇
Continuous exploration
Continuous integration

The method for gathering customer feedback that relies on building analytic systems to deliver information about how customers are using the Solution is "Telemetry." Telemetry involves collecting and analyzing data on how customers interact with the product or solution in real-time, often through embedded analytics and tracking mechanisms. This allows organizations to gain insights into user behavior, preferences, and potential issues, which can then be used to inform further development and enhancements.

Team A has seven developers that can define and build any application the organization requires. Team A works with another team to test and deploy their work. Can Team A be considered a high-functioning Agile Team?
Yes, because they can build any application the organization requires No, because they have fewer than ten developers No, because they are not cross-functional Yes, because they use another team to deploy

No, because they are not cross-functional.

A high-functioning Agile team ideally embodies the principle of being cross-functional, meaning it possesses all the skills necessary to define, build, and test its increments of work. In the scenario described, Team A relies on another team to test and deploy their work. This dependency indicates that Team A is not fully cross-functional, as it lacks the ability to perform testing and deployment within the team. In Agile methodologies, having self-sufficient teams that can take a piece of work from conception to delivery without relying on external teams for core activities is a key component of agility and rapid delivery of value.

9 / 45
Which of the following Story components captures details on testing for completion?
Release plan
User voice
Acceptance criteria
Story map

The component of a User Story that captures details on testing for completion is the "Acceptance criteria." Acceptance criteria are a set of predefined requirements that must be met for a story to be considered complete. They provide clear definitions of what is expected from the solution for it to be acceptable to the customer or product owner, including specific conditions that must be tested and satisfied.

During the innovation and Planning Iteration, an organization invites every team member to work on any project they choose. Which of the following SAFe Core Values is the organization demonstrating?
Visualizing work
Make value flow without interruptions
Siloed thinking
Relentless improvement

The scenario described illustrates the SAFe Core Value of "Relentless Improvement." By inviting every team member to work on any project they choose during the Innovation and Planning Iteration, the organization demonstrates a commitment to providing opportunities for growth, exploration, and continuous learning. This approach fosters an environment where team members can experiment with new ideas, learn from one another, and collaboratively work towards enhancing both personal and organizational capabilities. It underscores the value of relentless improvement in driving innovation and efficiency within the organization.

What is the purpose of the Iteration review?
To measure the team's progress
〇〇〇
To forecast where work is estimated for the upcoming Pis To identify where there is too much work in the system To work on solutions for backlog items

The purpose of the Iteration Review is to measure the team's progress by demonstrating the working software completed during the iteration to stakeholders. It provides an opportunity for the team to receive feedback on the increment developed, discuss what went well, what didn't, and how to improve in the next iteration. The Iteration Review is a critical event in Agile methodologies such as Scrum, where the focus is on inspecting and adapting the product increment based on stakeholder feedback, thereby ensuring that the team is building the right product and continuously improving the development process.

Which of the following is an output of the PI Planning process?
Actual PI Business Value
Pl Vision
• PI Goals
PI Objectives

An output of the PI (Program Increment) Planning process is "PI Objectives." PI Objectives are a set of summarizations derived from the team breakouts during PI Planning, which describe what each Agile Release Train (ART) intends to deliver in the upcoming Program Increment. They are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) outcomes that teams commit to achieving in the PI. These objectives are critical for aligning teams towards a common goal, providing a clear direction, and facilitating measurement of progress against the goals set during PI Planning.

What is the recommended final agenda item of PI Planning?
Reviewing the final plan
Participating in the planning retrospective
Surfacing the ART risks
Taking the Pl confidence vote

The recommended final agenda item of PI (Program Increment) Planning is "Taking the PI confidence vote." This step occurs after all planning activities, risk identification, and problem-solving efforts have been completed. The PI confidence vote is a method in which all participants express their confidence in achieving the PI Objectives based on the plan developed during the PI Planning event. This activity provides a collective sense of the team's readiness and commitment to the plan and identifies any remaining concerns that might need to be addressed.

Which of the following categories addresses potential risks?
Obtained
Resolved
Acquired
Managed

The category that addresses potential risks is "Managed." In the context of risk management, "managed" refers to the process of identifying, assessing, prioritizing, and implementing strategies to minimize, monitor, and control the probability or impact of uncertain events. Managing risks involves a proactive approach to dealing with potential problems before they occur or become actual issues, ensuring that they are kept within acceptable levels.

Which of the following basic quality practices applies to all teams?
Rapid prototyping
Collective ownership and standards
Modeling and simulation
Agile architecture

The basic quality practice that applies to all teams is "Collective ownership and standards." This principle emphasizes that quality is everyone's responsibility within the team. It highlights the importance of shared ownership of both the code base and the standards applied to it. By adhering to agreed-upon standards and taking collective ownership, teams ensure that the system or product is built to a high-quality level consistently across all parts of the development process. This approach facilitates seamless collaboration, enhances the maintainability of the code, and contributes to the overall quality of the final product.

Which of the following continuous delivery pipeline aspects focuses on enabling the organization to deliver value aligned with business needs?
Continuous Deployment
Release on Demand
Continuous Integration
Continuous Exploration

The aspect of the continuous delivery pipeline that focuses on enabling the organization to deliver value aligned with business needs is "Release on Demand." Release on Demand is the ability to make value available to customers when they want it and where they want it, based on business needs. It represents the ultimate decoupling of deployment to production from the release to users. This approach allows businesses to respond quickly to market changes, customer feedback, and competitive pressures by making new features and updates available at the most opportune time.

DED BY SCALED AGILE

Assessment progress:
SAFe Practitioner Exam (6.0)
Time remaining: 01.1
17 / 45
Which of the following SAFe Agile Team types relies on a continually-refined Team Backlog as the primary input to drive value delivery?
SAFe Team Kanban
SAFe Co-located Team
0〇
SAFe Lean Team
SAFe Epic Team

The SAFe Agile Team type that relies on a continually-refined Team Backlog as the primary input to drive value delivery is the "SAFe Team Kanban." Team Kanban is a method within the Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) that emphasizes continuous flow and delivery. It uses a Kanban board to visualize the state of work and focuses on just-in-time delivery of value through managing and refining the team backlog. This approach allows teams to adapt quickly to changes and prioritize work efficiently to ensure continuous delivery of value.

Which of the following Agile Team responsibillties is associated with the Iteration Retrospective?
Apply systems thinking
Improve relentlessly
Connect to the customer
Take an economic view

The Agile Team responsibility associated with the Iteration Retrospective is "Improve relentlessly." The Iteration Retrospective is a meeting held at the end of each iteration in Agile methodologies, such as Scrum, where the team reflects on the past iteration. They discuss what went well, what could be improved, and plan actions to make improvements in the next iteration. This practice is central to the principle of continuous improvement, encouraging teams to relentlessly seek ways to improve their processes, tools, relationships, and work products.

What is one way to understand current WIP in a system?
Split Stories
Make current work visible
Pair to complete the work faster
Size Stories smaller

One way to understand current Work In Progress (WIP) in a system is to "Make current work visible." By visually representing work items, for example, using Kanban boards or digital project management tools, teams and stakeholders can immediately see the amount and status of WIP. This visibility helps in managing flow, identifying bottlenecks, and ensuring that WIP limits are adhered to, facilitating smoother workflow and helping to enhance the efficiency of the system.

Which of the following roles act as proxies for the customer in representing their needs to the teams?
Product roles
Executive roles
Architecture roles
Developer roles

Product roles act as proxies for the customer in representing their needs to the teams. Specifically, in many Agile and SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) environments, the Product Owner role is crucial for this purpose. The Product Owner is responsible for defining stories, prioritizing the team backlog, and ensuring that the product or solution being developed meets the customer's needs and expectations. They serve as a direct liaison between the customer and the development team, ensuring that the voice of the customer is heard throughout the product development process.

Team A is writing a Story enabling book shoppers to access their shopping cart from any page on the website. Which of the folkoving examples represents the recommended user voice format for the Story?
I am a book shopper that wants to access my shopping cart anywhere on the website
As a book shopper, I want access to my shopping cart from any page, so that I can review what I am purchasing I want to view my shopping cart so I can review what 1 am purchasing As a book shopper, I want to access my shopping cart from any page

The recommended user voice format for the Story, based on Agile methodologies, is:

"As a book shopper, I want access to my shopping cart from any page, so that I can review what I am purchasing."

This format follows the standard user story template in Agile development: "As a [type of user], I want [some goal] so that [some reason]." This template is designed to keep the focus on the user's needs and the reasons behind those needs, facilitating the development of features that provide real value to the users.

Which of the following statements describes the balance between emergent design and intentional architecture when building in quality?
It is required for speed of value delivery and Solution Intent It is required for backlog speed and designed refinement
It is required for implementation speed and maturity
It is required for speed of development and maintainability

The statement that best describes the balance between emergent design and intentional architecture when building in quality is:

"It is required for speed of development and maintainability."

This balance ensures that while the design can evolve during the development process to adapt to new insights and requirements (emergent design), there is also a deliberate planning and structuring phase that addresses broader system concerns (intentional architecture). This equilibrium supports rapid development while also ensuring that the system is structured in a way that maintains its integrity, facilitates future enhancements, and allows for sustainable long-term maintenance.
